title: Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters
summary: The repository artifact is a local copy/version of U-M's 1999 ArderneFistula electronic text, keyed from the 1968 lithographic EETS reprint of D'Arcy Power's 1910 edition. Power prints the anonymous Middle English compilation in British Library Sloane MS 6, ff. 141r-174v, but supplies two substantial lacunae from the different English version in Sloane MS 277 and adds a charm from an unidentified source after Sloane 6 ends. A complete 427-canvas IIIF facsimile of the whole Sloane 6 codex and a complete PDF of the 1910 edition are publicly retrievable and cached locally with exact-source records.
review_notes: The Latin source writings, anonymous Middle English translations, physical manuscripts, Power's 1910 edition, its 1968 lithographic reprint, U-M encoding, repository artifact, and reproductions are separate layers.
Sloane MS 6 is the principal witness. Sloane MS 277 is a direct source only for passages Power explicitly substitutes where Sloane 6 is defective; it is not modeled as a universal parallel base.
The complete Sloane 6 cache covers all 427 IIIF canvases at a documented derivative request size. The cataloged compilation maps to ff. 141r-174v, canvases 289-356, within that full codex.
Current U-M No Copyright-United States language and the restrictive 1999 availability notice embedded in the XML are both preserved rather than silently reconciled.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for ArderneFistula
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The pinned submodule source is not modified by these manifests.

entities.label: University of Michigan ArderneFistula electronic text
entities.description: A manually keyed and structurally encoded electronic representation of Power's 1968 reprint, released through the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse.
notes: The XML says the text was keyboarded by a person named only as 'Chrisian' and was within one error per 20,000 characters based on review of 5 percent of the text.

The encoding includes the edition's front matter, core text, added charm, appendix, and notes. Despite the header's general claim that all material is included so far as possible, the printed index is absent and the five plates/figures are represented by captions rather than image data.

entities.label: Power 1968 lithographic reprint of Treatises of Fistula in Ano

bibliographic.citation: John Arderne, Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters, ed. D'Arcy Power, EETS Original Series 139 (Oxford: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society, lithographic reprint, 1968; first published 1910).
bibliographic.title: Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 139
bibliographic.publisher: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

notes: The reprint title page and colophon explicitly call this a lithographic reprint of the 1910 printing; no new editor or substantive revision is identified.

entities.label: Power 1910 EETS edition of Treatises of Fistula in Ano

bibliographic.citation: John Arderne, Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters, from an Early Fifteenth-Century Manuscript Translation, ed. D'Arcy Power, EETS Original Series 139 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. and Henry Frowde, Oxford University Press, 1910).
bibliographic.title: Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 139
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.; Henry Frowde, Oxford University Press

notes: Power prints Sloane 6 as the principal English witness, supplies two defective stretches from Sloane 277, and adds extensive modern introduction and notes.
After Sloane 6 stops abruptly at the end of p. 102, Power prints a charm on pp. 103-104 from an unnamed different source.

entities.label: British Library Sloane MS 6
entities.description: A second-quarter fifteenth-century English paper medical miscellany. The ten-part Middle English Arderne compilation occupies ff. 141r-174v within a 205-folio codex.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: second quarter of the fifteenth century (1425-1449)

holding.notes: The official component record locates Arderne's ten-part compilation at ff. 141r-174v and links the full digital facsimile.

physical_description.extent: 205 folios, plus three paper flyleaves at the beginning and three at the end; Arderne compilation at ff. 141r-174v
notes: Produced in England.
The codex contains numerous non-Arderne medical works before and after the cataloged portion; those adjacent contents are not part of this work.

entities.label: British Library Sloane MS 277
entities.description: A composite medical manuscript containing an English Practica de fistula in ano at ff. 60v-75v. Power describes it as another early-fifteenth-century English version and uses it to supply lacunae in Sloane 6.

date_statements.label: production of the English Arderne version
date_statements.value: early fifteenth century, according to Power
date_statements.notes: The current BL record gives only a broad 14th-17th-century range for the composite item, so Power's component-level dating is retained with attribution.

holding.notes: The official record locates the Arderne Practica at ff. 60v-75v and currently exposes no digitised content.

physical_description.extent: Arderne Practica at ff. 60v-75v within a composite medical manuscript
notes: The online record does not supply item-specific support, dimensions, or a narrow production date for this component.

entities.label: British Library complete digital facsimile of Sloane MS 6
entities.description: The complete 427-canvas IIIF Presentation manifest linked from the official BL catalog, covering the physical codex from front cover through spine.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest and Image API page images
notes: The local ZIP captures every canvas, normally at a requested maximum width of 1200 pixels, and retains the provider manifest and exact image-request inventory. The narrow terminal spine surface uses the service-native full profile after the requested derivative was refused; it is complete in canvas coverage, not a claim to preservation-master resolution.

entities.label: Internet Archive / Wellcome Library scan of Power's 1910 edition
entities.description: A complete page-image scan of the 1910 EETS volume, digitized in 2019 with Wellcome Library funding and exposed as PDF and original JP2 files.
entities.reproduction_format: Page images with PDF, JP2, OCR, and metadata derivatives

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
entities.description: Current official guidance on copyright duration used for the U.S. assessment of the 1910 publication.

agents.name: John Arderne
notes: Author of the Latin source writings; not the anonymous Middle English translator.

agents.name: Chrisian
notes: Recorded by this single name as the keyboarder in the XML header; no identity expansion is attempted.

scope.notes: The shared identifier, title, source statement, encoding declaration, and text establish the lineage; the exact date and mechanics of transfer into the CME git repository are undocumented.

subject.description: Electronic text, including front matter, core text, supplementary charm, appendix, and notes

object.description: Power's 1968 lithographic EETS reprint, excluding the printed index and the visual content of plates/figures

scope.notes: The XML source description names the 1968 reprint and says all material is included so far as possible. Structural inspection shows that the printed index is absent and FIGURE elements preserve captions rather than image data.
The 1968 colophon states that the 1910 book was reprinted lithographically; no substantive revision is identified.

subject.description: The main Middle English text on pp. 1-102 and its table, excluding the passages Power explicitly supplies from Sloane 277 and the added charm

object.description: The ten-part Arderne compilation in Sloane MS 6

scope.notes: Power's bibliography lists only the narrower fistula tract at ff. 141-154v; the current BL component description and visible manuscript table support ff. 141r-174v for the full ten-part compilation.

subject.description: Two passages replacing defective portions of Sloane 6

object.description: Readings from the different English Practica in Sloane 277
locators: Power first marks Sloane 277 at f. 66r

scope.notes: Power explicitly opens and closes both substitutions. A further Sloane 277 leaf marker on p. 32 is retained as evidence of an individual reading, not expanded into an undocumented third wholesale substitution.

subject.description: All 427 canvases in the BL IIIF Presentation manifest

object.description: The complete physical codex, including covers and spine

subject.description: Complete scanned-volume image set and derivatives

access.access_method: Read the public structured text and item information in U-M Digital Collections.
Open the Internet Archive item or retrieve the complete PDF directly; original page JP2 files and OCR derivatives are also listed in item metadata.

local_copies.label: Complete 1910 D'Arcy Power EETS edition PDF

notes: The PDF is a derivative of a complete page-image scan. Item metadata identifies Wellcome Library funding and applies an In Copyright rights statement despite the 1910 publication date; that provider assertion is retained separately.

access.access_method: Open the exact British Library item record.
Use the BL Universal Viewer or the exact IIIF Presentation manifest for all 427 canvases.

local_copies.label: Complete British Library Sloane MS 6 IIIF facsimile bundle
notes: Complete canvas coverage of the 427-image IIIF Presentation manifest; images are service derivatives requested at a maximum width of 1200 pixels, not preservation masters.
ZIP contains the provider manifest, an exact-source inventory, and all 427 canvas images listed when retrieved.

Effective IIIF image request profiles: '1200,' and 'full'; format: JPEG. The terminal spine canvas fell back to the service-native full profile, and the inventory records both the refused requested URL and successful exact URL.

work_portion.label: Middle English Arderne compilation edited by D'Arcy Power
notes: The official BL component description assigns John Arderne's ten-part Practica de fistula compilation to ff. 141r-174v; manifest labels map those folios to canvases 289-356.

access.format: IIIF Presentation 3 and Image API JPEG derivatives
notes: The ignored local cache is a research copy and is not itself evidence of permission to redistribute the BL photographs.

access.access_method: Registered readers may request the shelfmark through the BL collection-item request form; manuscripts must be consulted in London and the catalog states that a letter of introduction is required.
Open the exact British Library item record.
Registered readers may request Sloane MS 277 through the BL collection-item request form; the exact catalog record exposes no public digital surrogate.

rights.component: The 1910 transcription, introduction, notes, apparatus, typography, and included manuscript plates
rights.basis: The edition was first published in 1910. U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A states that works published in the United States before 1 January 1931 are in the public domain and describes a maximum 95-year term for pre-1978 statutory protection.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item ArderneFistula
rights.basis: The current item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: The embedded 1999 availability notice permits personal, research, and teaching copies but expressly forbids redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers; whether the current statement supersedes that notice is unresolved.

rights.component: The local U-M-derived XML transcription and markup
rights.basis: U-M currently labels the item public domain in the United States, but the checked-in XML retains an express redistribution restriction from 1999.

rights.component: Internet Archive's Wellcome-sourced scan and generated derivatives
rights.basis: The current Internet Archive item metadata applies the RightsStatements.org In Copyright statement, even though the underlying 1910 edition is public domain in the United States. The provider assertion is recorded without transferring it to the underlying medieval text or silently overriding the independent U.S. term analysis.

rights.component: British Library IIIF photographs of Sloane MS 6
rights.jurisdiction: Most countries other than the United Kingdom, as stated by the provider
rights.basis: The item-specific IIIF manifest states 'Public Domain in most countries other than the UK.'
notes: The statement is geographically qualified and is not restated as a worldwide license or dedication.

rights.component: British Library IIIF photographs of Sloane MS 6
rights.basis: The item-specific manifest expressly excludes the UK from its public-domain assertion and supplies no replacement item-level license for UK reuse.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Defective or missing leaves in the principal Sloane 6 text
editorial_practices.representation: Two marked stretches are supplied from the different English version in Sloane 277
editorial_practices.description: Power marks a first substitution on pp. 20-23 and a second on pp. 26-29, opening each with a note that Sloane 6 is defective and closing it when Sloane 6 resumes.
editorial_practices.scope: Middle English core text, printed pp. 20-23 and 26-29

editorial_practices.source_feature: Sloane 6 ends abruptly after the final remedy topic
editorial_practices.representation: Power adds a Latin and English charm from another unnamed source on pp. 103-104
editorial_practices.description: The charm is an edition-level supplement and is not attributed to Sloane 6 or Sloane 277 in this manifest.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete 1968 printed reprint, including editorial and supplementary matter
editorial_practices.representation: All material is included so far as possible in the electronic edition
editorial_practices.description: The U-M header explicitly says that all material is included so far as possible. The XML contains the front matter, core text, added charm, appendix, and notes, but omits the printed index and represents the five plates/figures with captions rather than image data.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Topic labels in Power's reproduced manuscript table where corresponding text headings are absent
editorial_practices.representation: Table labels are used as supplied topic headings in the encoded text
editorial_practices.description: The XML header warns that supplied headings follow the table and specifically records a discrepancy between table and text around mormales and piles.
editorial_practices.scope: Core text topic divisions, including the supplied heading near printed p. 64

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, ArderneFistula.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies the U-M encoding, 1968 source reprint, 1910 original, principal and supplementary witnesses, encoding quality statement, broad inclusion claim, supplied headings, legacy availability terms, and the text's structure. Structural inspection shows that the printed index is absent and FIGURE elements contain captions rather than image data.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item ArderneFistula.
evidence.summary: Current item record identifies the title, Arderne, Power, Oxford 1968 publication, structured contents, public digital text, and U-M's Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement.

evidence.citation: John Arderne, Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters, ed. D'Arcy Power, EETS Original Series 139 (London, 1910), complete Internet Archive scan b31350409.
evidence.locator: title page; Forewords pp. xii-xv and xxxiii-xxxv; table pp. xxxvi-xxxvii; pp. 20-29; notes pp. 130-135
evidence.summary: Direct inspection establishes Power's bibliographic facts and editorial method: early-fifteenth-century English translation, Sloane 6 principal text, Sloane 277 substitutions, ten-part/44-topic organization, 1376 Latin fistula date, post-1413 translation terminus, incomplete remedies, and an added charm from another source.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for item b31350409.
evidence.summary: Lists the complete PDF and original JP2 archive, identifies 2019 scanning with Wellcome Library funding, and records the item-specific In Copyright RightsStatements.org assertion.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Sloane MS 6, record 040-002112343.
evidence.locator: scope and content; ff. 141r-174v; digitised content; physical description; access
evidence.summary: Official item record dates and describes the complete codex, locates its production in England, assigns the ten-part Arderne compilation to ff. 141r-174v, links the full digital facsimile, and gives the physical request route.

evidence.citation: British Library IIIF Presentation 3 manifest for Sloane MS 6.
evidence.locator: manifest label, rights, metadata, and all 427 canvas entries
evidence.summary: The live manifest contains 427 canvases from front cover through spine, supplies exact Image API services and folio labels, attributes digitization to the British Library, and states 'Public Domain in most countries other than the UK.'

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of British Library Sloane MS 6, ff. 141r and 174v, through the item IIIF service.
evidence.summary: Folio 141r visibly opens with the 44-topic table headed as the extracted Arderne treatise; f. 174v contains the closing text of topic 44. This verifies the work-to-canvas boundary used in the complete-codex cache.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Sloane MS 277, record 040-002112623.
evidence.locator: scope and content; ff. 60v-75v; digitised content; access
evidence.summary: Official record locates the English Arderne Practica at ff. 60v-75v, exposes no digitized content, and provides the physical-item request route.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.summary: Current official guidance states that works published in the United States before 1 January 1931 are in the public domain and describes the maximum total 95-year term for pre-1978 statutory copyrights.

Provides the general contractual framework for BL online services while stating that service-specific terms take precedence; it does not replace the Sloane 6 manifest's geographically qualified item-level rights statement.

evidence.citation: Peter Murray Jones, 'John Arderne's Afterlife in Manuscript and Print,' in Genre in English Medical Writing, 1500-1820 (Cambridge University Press, 2022), pp. 13-31, doi:10.1017/9781009105347.003.
evidence.summary: Current specialist context confirms the continuing Latin and English manuscript and print tradition and identifies Jones's earlier studies of the four Middle English translations and Sloane witnesses.
notes: Only the public abstract and bibliography were consulted; the paywalled chapter is not used for precise derivation claims.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement supersede the 1999 XML notice that forbids redistribution by commercial and not-for-profit content providers?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for written clarification before redistributing the U-M-derived XML or a substantially copied encoding.
Treat the medieval text and 1910 edition separately from U-M's transcription and markup.

open_questions.question: Which manuscript supplied Power's Latin and English charm on pp. 103-104 after Sloane MS 6 ends?
next_steps: Consult Power's papers or later critical work for an explicit identification.
Do not attach the charm to Sloane 6 or Sloane 277 without evidence.

open_questions.question: What is the precise production date, physical description, and current digitization status of the Arderne component in Sloane MS 277?
next_steps: Request an item-level description or reproduction information from the British Library, quoting Sloane MS 277 and ff. 60v-75v.
Consult Peter Murray Jones's 1989 comparison of the Middle English translations.
notes: The official record currently has no digitised-content link; this negative finding is dated and is not proof that no institutional surrogate exists.

open_questions.question: What rights or permission govern reuse of the Sloane MS 6 IIIF photographs within the United Kingdom?
next_steps: Ask the British Library for an item-specific UK reuse statement before publishing the cached photographs.
Preserve the manifest's exact non-UK qualification and do not infer a worldwide public-domain dedication.
